Moist.description: Moist. The companion gains a Swim Speed equal to its Speed, and it can move through a space as narrow as 1 inch without expending extra movement to do so. In addition, whenever the companion is hit by an attack roll from a creature within 10 feet of itself, the attacker takes Acid damage equal to your Intelligence modifier.

Acid Retaliation.name: Acid Retaliation
Acid Retaliation.condition: hit by an attack from a target in range

Moist.name: Moist

Bloated.description: Bloated. The companion becomes Large. Whenever it hits a Large or smaller creature with its Dreadful Swipe action, that creature is pushed up to 10 feet away from the companion. Additionally, you can add your Intelligence modifier to the damage dealt by the companion’s Death Burst.
Bloated.name: Bloated

Brute Spirit.name: Brute Spirit
Brute Spirit.description: Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the target makes a [[/save]]{Strength saving throw.} On a failed save, a creature takes [[/damage]]{Thunder damage} equal to three rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die and has the &Reference[Prone] condition. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only.

Everlasting.description: 你不會因脫水、飢餓或窒息獲得力竭等級。你無需睡眠，魔法也無法使你陷入睡眠。你可以保持非活動的靜止狀態來僅用4小時完成長休，且在這期間保持意識清醒。
Everlasting.name: 永固不朽

Life Essence.description: While you have at least one soul trinket, you have Advantage on Death Saving Throws and Constitution saving throws.
Life Essence.name: Life Essence

Death's Knell.name: Death's Knell
Death's Knell.condition: you deal Sneak Attack damage on your turn

Soul Trinkets.name: Soul Trinkets
Soul Trinkets.description:
Using Soul Trinkets. You can use soul trinkets in the following ways:

Death’s Knell. When you deal Sneak Attack damage on your turn, you can destroy one soul trinket and immediately use Wails from the Grave without expending a use of that feature.

Life Essence. While you have at least one soul trinket, you have Advantage on Death Saving Throws and Constitution saving throws.

Spirit Query. You can take a Magic action to destroy one soul trinket and immediately cast the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplAugury0000]{Augury} spell, requiring no spell components and using Constitution as the spellcasting modifier. If you know the creature with which the trinket is associated, you can ask the creature’s spirit one question instead of casting the spell. In this case, the spirit appears to you and answers as concisely as possible in a language it knew in life.

Foundry Note

The Life Essence active effect should be disabled when this item's limited uses are exhausted. Alternatively, the effect will be disabled if the Soul Trinkets consumable is unequipped.

Avenger Spirit.name: Avenger Spirit
Avenger Spirit.description: Until the end of your next turn, any creature that hits the target with a melee attack roll takes [[/damage]]{Force damage} equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die.

Ghost Walk.name: Ghost Walk
Ghost Walk.description:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwPRGhostWalkKY inline]

Hazy Form and Incorporeal Movement is not automated.

Ghost Walk.name: Ghost Walk
Ghost Walk.description:
As a Bonus Action, you assume a spectral form, gaining the benefits below for 10 minutes or until you end them (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you destroy one of your soul trinkets from Tokens of the Departed (no action required) to restore your use of it.

Flight. You gain a Fly Speed of 10 feet and
can hover.

Hazy Form. Attack rolls have Disadvantage against you.

Incorporeal Movement. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were &Reference[Difficult Terrain], but you take [[/damage 1d10 Force]] damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.

Jolt to Life.description:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSparetheDy caption=false]

Jolt to Life. When you cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSparetheDy]{Spare the Dying}, you can modify the spell so that it sends a jolt of electricity through the target, reviving it. The target regains a number of Hit Points equal to your Artificer level, and each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from the target makes a [[/save]]{Dexterity saving throw} against your spell save DC, taking [[/damage]]{2d4 Lightning} damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

You can modify the spell this way a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. The Lightning damage of this feature increases by 1d4 when you reach Artificer levels 11 (3d4) and 17 (4d4).
Jolt to Life.name: Jolt to Life

Lightning Emanation.condition: as part of casting this cantrip
Lightning Emanation.name: Lightning Emanation

Grave Touched.name: Grave Touched

Undead Endurance.hint: You don’t gain &Reference[Exhaustion apply=false] levels from &Reference[dehydration apply=false], &Reference[malnutrition apply=false], or &Reference[suffocation apply=false]. In addition, you don’t need to sleep, and magic can’t put you to sleep.
Undead Endurance.title: Undead Endurance

Grave Touched.description:
Your patron’s powers have a profound effect on your body and magic, granting you the following benefits.

Arcane Necrosis. Necrotic damage from your attacks, Warlock spells, and Warlock features ignores Resistance to Necrotic damage. Once per turn when you cast a spell that deals damage, you can change that spell’s damage type to Necrotic.

Dreaded Necrosis. When you hit a creature with an attack roll and deal Necrotic damage while using your Form of Dread, you can roll one additional damage die when determining the Necrotic damage the target takes. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

Undead Endurance. You don’t gain &Reference[Exhaustion apply=false] levels from &Reference[dehydration apply=false], &Reference[malnutrition apply=false], or &Reference[suffocation apply=false]. In addition, you don’t need to sleep, and magic can’t put you to sleep.

Foundry Note

Arcane and Dreaded Necrosis effects are not automated.

Unearthly Scream.condition: when you are hit by an attack roll
Unearthly Scream.name: Unearthly Scream

Voices from Beyond.name: Voices from Beyond
Voices from Beyond.condition: immediately after rolling a 1 on a D20 Test

Voices from Beyond (Deafened).name: Voices from Beyond (Deafened)
Voices from Beyond (Deafened).description:
While Deafened, you have Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Disadvantage on attack rolls is not automated.

Gathered Whispers.description:
You are haunted by a cacophony of whispering spirits only you can hear. You gain the following features.

Spirit Whispers. You learn the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplMessage000]{Message} spell and can cast it without Material components. Additionally, you always have the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplAugury0000]{Augury} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have.

Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this benefit (choose when you select this feat).

Unearthly Scream. When you are hit by an attack roll, you can take a Reaction to channel your haunting spirits into a protective, otherworldly scream. You can add your Proficiency Bonus to your AC against that attack, potentially causing it to miss. You can use this benefit a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Voices from Beyond. Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, the haunting voices rise to a ghastly volume. Make a [[/save]]{Wisdom saving throw} (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the Deafened condition until the end of your next turn. While Deafened, you have Disadvantage on ability checks and attack rolls.

Spirit Whispers.hint: You always have the Augury spell prepared.
Spirit Whispers.title: Spirit Whispers

Gathered Whispers.name: Gathered Whispers

Choose Languages.hint: Your character knows at least three languages: Common plus two languages you roll or choose from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.content.JournalEntry.phbCreatingAChar.JournalEntryPage.t1WP9VEevo4TOTXw#choose-languages]{Standard Languages} table.
Choose Languages.title: Choose Languages

Feat.title: Feat
Feat.hint: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSharpEyev8PBf]{Sharp Eye} or a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice.

Investigator.description:
Ability Scores: Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma

Feat: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSharpEyev8PBf]{Sharp Eye} or a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice.

Skill Proficiencies: Insight and Investigation

Tool Proficiency: Disguise Kit

Equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Disguise Kit, Manacles, Shovel, Traveler’s Clothes, 3 Vials, and 16 GP; or (B) 50 GP

You relentlessly seek the truth. Perhaps you witnessed something remarkable or terrible and now desire to unravel its mystery, or maybe you’re motivated by universal justice and honesty. Whether the cases you’re embroiled in are local crimes or eldritch conspiracies, you’re driven to reveal what others would keep hidden.
Investigator.name: Investigator

Choose Languages.hint: Your character knows at least three languages: Common plus two languages you roll or choose from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.content.JournalEntry.phbCreatingAChar.JournalEntryPage.t1WP9VEevo4TOTXw#choose-languages]{Standard Languages} table.
Choose Languages.title: Choose Languages

Feat.hint: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice (see “Feats” later in this chapter; @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwGatheredWhisp]{Gathered Whispers} is recommended)
Feat.title: Feat

Spirit Medium.description:
Ability Scores: Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom

Feat: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice (@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwGatheredWhisp]{Gathered Whispers} is recommended)

Skill Proficiencies: Insight and Religion

Tool Proficiency: Choose one kind of Gaming Set

Equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Dagger, Gaming Set (same as above), Basket, Bell, 8 Candles, Ink, Ink Pen, Paper (5 sheets), Tinderbox, Traveler’s Clothes, and 32 GP; or (B) 50 GP

Through strange rituals and fateful encounters, you’ve discovered that you bear a unique connection to the spirits of the dead and damned. Your body is a conduit for such spirits, granting you prescient insight from beyond. But these spirits’ intentions aren’t always innocent, and their gifts come at a haunting cost.
Spirit Medium.name: Spirit Medium

Create Companion.condition: using a type of Artisan's Tools with which you have proficiency
Create Companion.name: Create Companion

Reanimated Companion.name: Reanimated Companion
Reanimated Companion.description:
Using Tinker’s Tools or another type of Artisan’s Tools with which you have proficiency, you can take a Magic action to create a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.FFTZLIEaYBc8sQfW]{Reanimated Companion} through the power of necromancy and science. The companion manifests in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. You determine the companion’s appearance; your choices don’t affect the companion’s game statistics.

The companion is Friendly to you and your allies and obeys you. It lasts until you finish a Long Rest or until you take a Magic action to dismiss it early, at which point it harmlessly collapses into a pile of viscera. It immediately drops to 0 Hit Points and dies (triggering its Death Burst trait) if you die.

Once you create a companion, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest or expend a spell slot to create one. You can have only one companion at a time and can’t create one while your companion is present.

The Companion in Combat. In combat, the companion acts during your turn. It can move and take its Reaction on its own, but the only action it takes is the Dodge action unless you take a Bonus Action to command it to take an action. If you have the Incapacitated condition, the companion acts on its own and isn’t limited to the Dodge action.

Shadow Sorcery.name: Shadow Sorcery
Shadow Sorcery.description:
Bend Doom and Darkness to Your Will

Your innate magic comes from the most nebulous and inscrutable forces of the Shadowfell or from other regions of supernatural darkness. You might trace your lineage to an entity from such a place, or perhaps you were exposed to the sinister energy of a shadow dragon and were transformed by it. Your shadowy magic allows you to command darkness, undeath, and woe.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.ShadowSorcerySor]{Subclass Details}

Intrusive Echos.name: Intrusive Echos
Intrusive Echos.condition: immediately after rolling a 1 on a D20 Test

Channeled Prowess (Expertise).title: Channeled Prowess (Expertise)
Channeled Prowess (Expertise).hint: In addition, choose one skill you have proficiency in. You gain Expertise in that skill. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your choice for this benefit.

Intrusive Echoes (Incapacitated).description: You have the &Reference[Incapacitated apply=false] condition until the end of your next turn. While you are Incapacitated in this way, your Speed is halved.
Intrusive Echoes (Incapacitated).name: Intrusive Echoes (Incapacitated)

Echoing Soul.description:
You experience echoes from a past or alternate life. You gain the following features.

Channeled Prowess. You have proficiency in two skills of your choice.

In addition, choose one skill you have proficiency in. You gain Expertise in that skill. Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can change your choice for this benefit.

Inherent Tongues. You know one additional language of your choice, which you choose from the language tables in the Player’s Handbook.

Intrusive Echoes. Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, memories and sensations from your soul’s other life threaten to overtake you. Make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the Incapacitated condition until the end of your next turn. While you are Incapacitated in this way, your Speed is halved.
Echoing Soul.name: Echoing Soul

Reborn.name: 重生者
Reborn.description:
重生者是指那些已經死去卻又莫名依然存活的個體。有些重生者的身上有著致命傷留下的疤痕，那死灰般的血肉和無血的血管無不表明著他們曾死裡逃生。有些重生者則是魔法或科學造就的奇蹟，他們或由迥然不同的生命縫合而成，或是在人造軀殼中承載著神秘的心智。無論起源如何，重生者都迎來了新生，並尋求屬於他們自己的經歷和答案。

重生者特性

生物類型：類人生物

體型：中型（約4-7呎高）或小型（約2-4呎高），當你選擇此種族時決定

速度：30呎

作為重生者，你擁有這些特性

【死裡逃生】@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwEscapedDeatXY inline]

【永固不朽】@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwEverlasting7o inline]

【往昔知識】 @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwKnowledgefraL inline]

【奇異耐性】你具有下列一項你所選擇的傷害類型抗性：寒冷、黯蝕、毒素。

Strange Endurance.hint: 你具有下列一項你所選擇的傷害類型抗性：寒冷、黯蝕、毒素。
Strange Endurance.title: 奇異耐性

Umt70RQ304hjOibr.hint: 中型（約4-7呎高）或小型（約2-4呎高），當你選擇此種族時決定

Spirits from Beyond.name: Spirits from Beyond
Spirits from Beyond.description:
You can call forth spirits of the dead to empower you and your allies. When you take a Bonus Action to give a creature a Bardic Inspiration die, you can call forth the powers of a random spirit. To determine the spirit you channel, roll the Bardic Inspiration die and refer to the Spirits from Beyond table. The spirit remains channeled until you unleash it or until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Controlled Channeling. As a Bonus Action, you can expend a use of your Bardic Inspiration and channel a specific spirit. When you do so, choose the spirit from the Spirits from Beyond table rather than rolling. The chosen spirit’s corresponding number must be less than or equal to the highest number on your Bardic Inspiration die; for example, if your Bardic Inspiration die is a d8, you can choose to channel any spirit up to (and including) the Shade.

Unleashing a Spirit. As a Magic action, you can unleash one of your channeled spirits. Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself as the spirit’s target. The spirit then takes effect; if a spirit’s effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals your Bard spell save DC.

Spirits from Beyond

Divine Reaper.name: Divine Reaper
Divine Reaper.description:
Your deep connection to this domain renders you a hallowed harbinger of death, granting you the following benefits.

Enhanced Necromancy. When you cast a spell of level 5 or lower from the Necromancy school that targets one creature, or when you cast a spell from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwCSGraveDomaZF]{Grave Domain Spells} table, you can expend a use of Channel Divinity to target a second creature within the spell’s range. If the spell requires costly or consumed Material components, you must provide Material components for each target.

Keeper of Souls. When an enemy dies within 60 feet of you, you or one creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself [[/heal]]{regains Hit Points} equal to twice your Cleric level. You can’t use this feature if you have the Incapacitated condition. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you expend a level 6+ spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.

Unleash.duration: while the target has these Temporary Hit Points
Unleash.name: Unleash

Wayfarer Spirit.name: Wayfarer Spirit
Wayfarer Spirit.description: The target gains Temporary Hit Points equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die plus your Bard level. While the target has these Temporary Hit Points, its Speed increases by 10 feet.

Skill or Tool Proficiency.title: Skill or Tool Proficiency
Skill or Tool Proficiency.hint: You may re-run this Advancement after a Short or Long Rest to select a different proficiency.

Whispers of the Dead.description: Whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest, you can choose one skill or tool proficiency that you lack and gain it, as a ghostly presence shares its knowledge with you. You lose this proficiency when you use this benefit again to choose a different proficiency.
Whispers of the Dead.name: Whispers of the Dead

Vampiric Bite.title: Vampiric Bite
Vampiric Bite.hint: When you use your Unarmed Strike and deal damage, you can choose to bite with your fangs.

Spider Climb Improvement.hint: When you reach character level 3, you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings while leaving your hands free.
Spider Climb Improvement.title: Spider Climb Improvement

yoBxJFhtJX5a9P7q.hint: Medium (about 4–7 feet tall) or Small (about 2–4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species.

Dhampir.name: Dhampir
Dhampir.description:
Dhampirs are living people who possess vampiric prowess but are cursed with macabre hunger. Most dhampirs thirst for blood, but some gain sustenance from dreams, life energy, or other vital sources. Dhampirs must choose whether to fight to control their hunger or give in to predatory urges.

Some dhampirs are the descendants of a powerful vampire, while others are partially transformed by a vampire’s bite. Macabre bargains and necromantic influences might also give rise to dhampirs.

Dhampir Traits

Creature Type: Humanoid

Size: Medium (about 4–7 feet tall) or Small (about 2–4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

Speed: 35 feet

As a Dhampir, you have these special traits.

Darkvision. You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Trace of Undeath. You have Resistance to Necrotic damage.

Spider Climb. @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSpiderClimb7F inline]

Vampiric Bite. @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwVampiricBitnE inline]

damage.condition: you deal Sneak Attack damage on your turn

Wails from the Grave.description:
Immediately after you deal @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.classes.Item.phbrgeSneakAttac]{Sneak Attack} damage to a creature on your turn, you can target a second creature that you can see within 30 feet of the first creature. Roll half the number of Sneak Attack damage dice for your level (round up), and the second creature takes [[/damage]]{Necrotic damage} equal to the roll’s total as wails of the dead sound around it.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Wails from the Grave.name: Wails from the Grave

Reanimator.name: Reanimator
Reanimator.description:
Reassemble Corpses and Raise the Dead

Reanimators defy the laws of nature in pursuit of gruesome experiments. These grim Artificers stitch together servants from disparate corpses, use foul magic to strengthen the living, and transform the art of necromancy into a terrifying science.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.ReanimatorArtifi]{Subclass Details}

Bonus Spell Damage.condition: deal damage with an Artificer spell
Bonus Spell Damage.name: Bonus Spell Damage

Arcane Conduit.description: Arcane Conduit. You can cast spells as though you were in the companion’s space, but you must use your own senses. Once per turn, when you cast an Artificer spell from the Evocation or Necromancy school and deal damage while your companion is within 120 feet of you, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell.
Arcane Conduit.name: Arcane Conduit

Pull of Death.condition: 對失去任何生命值的目標造成傷害時
Pull of Death.name: 死亡牽引

Circle of Mortality.name: 凡命循環
Circle of Mortality.description:
你可以操弄生與死的天平，這賦予你下列好處。

【死亡牽引】每回合一次，當你透過法術或攻擊檢定對一個失去任何生命值的生物造成傷害時，該生物承受額外1d4 黯蝕傷害。當你達到牧師 11 級時，此額外傷害提升為 1d6。

【回歸生命】你可以以附贈動作施放@UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSparetheDy]。

此外，當你對 0 HP 生物施放任何會恢復生命值的法術或引導神力時，你可以不擲骰改為每個治療骰取最大值。例如：2d4 治療變為固定恢復 8 點生命值。

Macabre Modifications.name: Macabre Modifications
Macabre Modifications.description:
You experiment and alter your companion further. Whenever you create a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.FFTZLIEaYBc8sQfW]{Reanimated Companion}, the companion now gains two options for Strange Modifications, instead of one. Additionally, when picking options for Strange Modifications, you can also choose from the following options.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSMBloated8heS inline]{Bloated}

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSMGauntb5vfDl inline]{Gaunt}

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSMMoistFP81Qo inline]{Moist}

Undead Patron.description:
Defy Death for Profane Power

You’ve made a pact with a creature that defies the cycle of life and death: a powerful lich, a vampire, or another entity of undeath. Having once been mortal, these ancient patrons know firsthand the paths of ambition and the routes past the doors of death. They eagerly share this profane knowledge and other secrets with those who work their will among the living.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.UndeadPatronWarl]{Subclass Details}
Undead Patron.name: Undead Patron

Beloved Spirit.name: Beloved Spirit
Beloved Spirit.description: The target [[/heal]]{regains Hit Points} equal to a roll of your Bardic Inspiration die plus your Charisma modifier.

Voice of Death.description:
You can cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSpeakwithD]{Speak with Dead} once without a spell slot, requiring no spell components and using Dexterity as the spellcasting modifier. You regain the ability to cast it this way when you finish a Short or Long Rest.

When you cast the spell, you can target one of your @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwPRSoulTrinkg0]{soul trinkets} from @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwPRTokensoftit]{Tokens of the Departed} instead of a corpse, allowing the spirit of the creature associated with the trinket to answer.
Voice of Death.name: Voice of Death

JAnvPaYxOgRXghpA.target: a corpse or a soul trinket

Superior Dread.description:
Your Form of Dread improves, granting you the following benefits while you are using it.

Dread Resistance. You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

Ghostly Flight. You have a Fly Speed equal to your Speed and can hover. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, but you take [[/damage 1d10 Force]] damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.

Profane Casting. Whenever you cast a Warlock spell from the Conjuration or Necromancy school, you cast it without any Verbal, Somatic, or Material components, except Material components that are costly or consumed.

Foundry Note

Profane Casting is not automated.
Superior Dread.name: Superior Dread

Create Token.name: Create Token
Create Token.duration: until you finish a Long Rest

Eerie Token.description:
As a Bonus Action, you can create a magical token by harmlessly removing a lock of hair, detaching a nail, or using some other method. While the token exists, you gain the following benefits:

  • Distant Message. As a Magic action, you can send a telepathic message of 25 words or fewer to a creature holding or carrying the token, as long as you are within 10 miles of it.

  • Remote Viewing. If you are within 10 miles of the token, you can take a Magic action to extend your senses through the token for 1 minute, until you have the Incapacitated condition, or until you end this state (no action required). During this state, you can see and hear from the token as if you were located where it is. When this state ends, the token is harmlessly destroyed.

Unless the token is destroyed early, it lasts until you finish a Long Rest. Once you create a token using this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
Eerie Token.name: Eerie Token

Coward Spirit.description: The target and each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from the target must succeed on a [[/save]]{Wisdom saving throw} or have the &Reference[Frightened apply=false] condition until the start of your next turn. While a creature is Frightened, its Speed is halved (round down), and it can take either an action or a Bonus Action, not both.
Coward Spirit.name: Coward Spirit

Hungering Might.name: Hungering Might
Hungering Might.condition: once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll while transformed

Frightened (Menacing Aura).name: Frightened (Menacing Aura)
Frightened (Menacing Aura).description: Menacing Aura. When a creature fails its saving throw against your Unnerving Aura, it also can’t regain Hit Points or take Reactions until the start of your next turn.

Wrath of the Wild.description:
You draw power from the strange and ancient horrors of the land, causing you to sprout unnatural growths, such as bloody antlers or putrid fangs, or causing your shadow to lengthen or twist around you. As a Bonus Action, you can expend a use of @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.classes.Item.phbrgrFavoredEne]{Favored Enemy} to transform into a ghastly form, gaining the following benefits for 1 minute or until you have the Incapacitated condition, die, or end the transformation (no action required).

Ancient Armor. You gain a +1 bonus to AC, as your body is wreathed in rotten bark and beastly bristles. This bonus increases to +2 when you reach Ranger level 11.

Prowling Retribution. Immediately after a creature you can see within 5 feet of yourself deals damage to you or one of your allies, you can make an Opportunity Attack against that creature.

Unnerving Aura. When you transform and at the start of each of your subsequent turns, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot Emanation originating from you makes a [[/save]]{Wisdom saving throw} against your spell save DC. On a failed save, a creature has the &Reference[Frightened apply=false] condition until the start of your next turn.

Foundry Note

The Prowling Retribution benefit is not automated.
Wrath of the Wild.name: Wrath of the Wild

Renegade Spirit.description: The target can immediately take a Reaction to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space it can see.
Renegade Spirit.name: Renegade Spirit

Incessant Watchers.name: Incessant Watchers
Incessant Watchers.condition: immediately after rolling a 1 on a D20 Test

Heightened Suspicion.condition: whenever you take the Search action
Heightened Suspicion.name: Heightened Suspicion

Watchers.description:
Something unnatural is always watching you, taking the form of scurrying vermin and other eerie creatures. You gain the following features.

Borrowed Eyes. You always have the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplBeastSense]{Beast Sense} and @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSpeakwithA]{Speak with Animals} spells prepared. You can cast each spell without a spell slot, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast these spells using spell slots you have of the appropriate level.

Heightened Suspicion. Whenever you take the Search action, you can roll [[/r 1d4]] and add the number rolled to any ability check made as part of that action.

Incessant Watchers. You have Disadvantage on saving throws made against the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplScrying000]{Scrying} spell.

In addition, immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, paranoia threatens to overwhelm you. Make a [[/save]]{Wisdom saving throw} (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have Disadvantage on D20 Tests for 1 minute. You can repeat the save at the end of each of your turns, ending the effect early on a success.

Paranoia.name: Paranoia
Paranoia.description: Disadvantage on attack rolls is not automated.

Watchers.name: Watchers

Hollow Warden.name: Hollow Warden
Hollow Warden.description:
Draw on the Might of Ancient Wild Terrors

Legends tell that the most ancient and bloodthirsty terrors lurk deep within the old places of the earth. Hollow Wardens venerate and draw power from such beings, transforming themselves into merciless and monstrous guardians that stalk jagged coastlines, steep mountain crags, and other dark and wild places.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.HollowWardenRang]{Subclass Details}

Expend Use on Success.name: Expend Use on Success
Expend Use on Success.condition: When you take Advantage on a specific action and succeed

Sharp Eye.name: Sharp Eye
Sharp Eye.description: When you take the &Reference[Search] or &Reference[Study] action, you can give yourself Advantage on any ability check made as part of that action. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. If the check fails, the use of this feature isn’t expended.

Lupin.description:
With their bristling fur and snarling muzzles, lupins cast imposing shadows in the Mists. Lupins often arise from encounters with werewolves in which the curse of lycanthropy didn’t fully take hold. Unlike true werewolves, lupins can’t shift between wolf and humanoid form but are instead stuck as a hybrid transformation. This cursed wolflike physiology affords lupins bestial might, enhanced instincts, and a dread-inducing howl.

Lupin Traits

Creature Type: Humanoid

Size: Medium (about 4–7 feet tall) or Small (about 2–4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

Speed: 30 feet

As a Lupin, you have these special traits.

Darkvision. You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Feral Pounce. @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwFeralPounce7E inline]

Howl. @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwHowlA3f2I58L3 inline]

Werewolf Instincts. You have proficiency in the Perception, Stealth, or Survival skill.

Power of Shadow.name: Power of Shadow
Power of Shadow.description:
You gain the following benefits.

Eyes of the Dark. You have Darkvision with a range of 120 feet and Blindsight with a range of 10 feet. In addition, if a spell you cast creates an area of Darkness, you can see normally through that spell’s Darkness.

Strength of the Grave. If you would drop to 0 Hit Points and not die outright, you can make a [[/save]]{Charisma saving throw} (DC 5 plus the damage taken). On a successful save, your Hit Points instead [[/heal]]{change to a number} equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Sorcerer level. After you succeed on this save, you can’t use this benefit again until you finish a Long Rest.

Touch of Death.name: Touch of Death
Touch of Death.description:
Deathly power resides within you, bursting out at the slightest provocation. You gain the following features.

Death Touch. You learn the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplChillTouch]{Chill Touch} spell and can cast it without spell components. Necrotic damage you deal with this spell ignores Resistance. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

Pull of the Grave. You have Disadvantage on Death Saving Throws.

Steel Yourself.name: Steel Yourself
Steel Yourself.condition: You fail a saving throw to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition

Survivor.description:
You gain the following benefits.

Hypervigilance. Whenever you roll Initiative, you can reroll the d20 if the number rolled is 9 or lower. You must use the new roll.

Steel Yourself. When you fail a saving throw to avoid or end the Charmed or Frightened condition, you can take a Reaction to add a bonus to the roll, potentially causing it to succeed. The bonus is equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

Once you take this Reaction, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest.
Survivor.name: Survivor

Improved Reanimation.name: Improved Reanimation
Improved Reanimation.description: The damage of your @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.FFTZLIEaYBc8sQfW]{Reanimated Companion’s} Death Burst increases to 4d4. Necrotic damage dealt by your companion ignores Resistance.

Shade Spirit.description: The target has the Invisible condition until the end of its next turn or until the target makes an attack roll, deals damage, or casts a spell. When the invisibility ends, each creature in a 5-foot Emanation originating from the target must succeed on a [[/save]]{Constitution saving throw} or take [[/damage]]{Necrotic damage} equal to two rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die.

Arsonist Spirit.description: The target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking Fire damage equal to four rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.

As a Bonus Action, you transform into an avatar of your patron’s dreadful power, gaining the benefits below for 1 minute, until you have the Incapacitated condition, or until you end the form (no action required). You can transform a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Facsimile of Life. You gain Temporary Hit Points equal to 1d10 plus your Warlock level.

Fearless Form. You have Immunity to the Frightened condition. If you are Frightened when you transform, the condition immediately ends for you.

Frightful Avatar. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack roll, you can force it to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the target has the Frightened condition until the end of your next turn.

Death's Friend.description:
Your association with death has become so close that you gain the following benefits.

Death’s Lament. When you use @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwPRWailsfromLz]{Wails from the Grave}, you can deal the feature’s Necrotic damage to both the first and the second creature.

Draw of Death. When you roll Initiative, you gain one soul trinket for your Tokens of the Departed if you have none remaining.

In addition, when you hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use both the Damage and the Shove options. You can use this benefit only once per turn.

Hexblood.description:
Hexbloods are individuals infused with fey magic as a result of eldritch energy or mysterious witchcraft. Many hexbloods are transformed into such beings after making a deal with a powerful hag, but other strange forces can result in the birth of a hexblood. A hexblood’s strange fey power manifests as eerie clairvoyance and beguiling magic.

Hexblood Traits

Creature Type: Fey

Size: Medium (about 4–7 feet tall) or Small (about 2–4 feet tall), chosen when you select this species

Speed: 30 feet

As a Hexblood, you have these special traits.

Darkvision. You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

Eerie Token. @Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwEerieTokenSri inline]

Hex Magic. Living Shadow.description:
The shadow you cast is animate and ever-present—sometimes it even acts according to its own will. You gain the following features.

Grasping Shadow. You learn the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplMageHand00]{Mage Hand} spell and can cast it without spell components. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

Lengthened Strike. When you make a melee attack roll as part of the Attack or Magic action on your turn, you can increase your reach for that attack by 10 feet, as your shadow stretches to aid you. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Ominous Will. Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, your shadow attempts to exert its will. Make a [[/save]]{Wisdom saving throw} (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the &Reference[Incapacitated apply=false] condition until the start of your next turn, at which point you must roll on the Shadow’s Will table to determine what you do during that turn.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwShadowsWillqh rollable resultLabel="Behavior" classes="caption-top"]{Shadow's Will}

Knowledge from a Past Life.description:
你獲得一項你所選擇的技能熟練。

此外，你可以暫時窺探過去來助你應付現在。當你屬性檢定失敗時，你可以擲1d6並將骰值加入d20中，這可能使該次失敗改為成功。你可以如此做的次數等於你的熟練加值，當你完成長休時，你重獲所有已消耗的使用次數。
Knowledge from a Past Life.name: 往昔知識

Grave Domain.description:
安息亡者，顯化神力

墳墓領域注重生與死的邊界。對從這一領域獲取神力的牧師來說，死亡是多元宇宙中自然且不可避免的一環。 這些牧師致力於摧毀亡靈，並超度亡魂踏入往生。

此領域的魔法同樣讓牧師能夠暫時將死亡拒之門外。 但這不過是暫緩死亡的到來，絕非徹底的拒絕——因為墳墓，終將收取它的貢賦。

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.GraveDomainCleri]{子職業細節}
Grave Domain.name: 墳墓領域

Aberrant Anatomy.description:
Exposure to alien horrors like those of the Far Realm has warped your physical form in supernatural ways. You gain the following features.

Breathless. You can hold your breath for 1 hour.

Extrasensory Perception. You have proficiency in the Perception skill, if you lack it. You also gain Expertise in that skill.

In addition, you have &Reference[Blindsight] with a range of 15 feet.

Warping Flesh. Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, the aberrant influence infecting your form flares, wrenching control of your flesh. Make a [[/save]]{Constitution saving throw} (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the &Reference[Stunned apply=false] condition until the end of your next turn.

Strange Modifications.name: Strange Modifications
Strange Modifications.description:
Whenever you create a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.FFTZLIEaYBc8sQfW]{Reanimated Companion,} it gains one of the following options of your choice; choose when you create the companion.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSMArcaneConVv inline]

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSMFerocityuzY inline]

Refined Reanimation.name: Refined Reanimation

Life Transfer.condition: you or your companion takes damage
Life Transfer.name: Life Transfer

Refined Reanimation.description:
You have mastered the science of revivification, granting you the following benefits.

Facilitated Revival. You can cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplRaiseDead0]{Raise Dead} once without expending a spell slot and without Material components, provided you use Tinker’s Tools or another type of Artisan’s Tools with which you have proficiency as the Spellcasting Focus. Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

Life Transfer. You can siphon the animating magic of your @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.FFTZLIEaYBc8sQfW]{Reanimated Companion} to bolster yourself. When you or your companion takes damage, you can take a Reaction to gain a number of [[/heal]]{Hit Points} equal to your companion’s current Hit Points. The companion then immediately drops to 0 Hit Points and dies (triggering its @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.actors.Actor.rhwReanimatedCom.Item.Inop9jX7p5oWLG8h]{Death Burst} trait).

Superior Modifications. Whenever you create a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.FFTZLIEaYBc8sQfW]{Reanimated Companion}, the companion now gains three options for @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwREStrangeMok1]{Strange Modifications.}

Umbral Form.description:
When you use @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.classes.Item.phbscrInnateSorc]{Innate Sorcery}, you can adopt a shadowy form, gaining the benefits below while your Innate Sorcery is active or until you end the form (no action required). Once you use this feature, you can’t use it again until you finish a Long Rest unless you spend 6 Sorcery Points (no action required) to restore your use of it.

Incorporeal Movement. You can move through creatures and objects as if they were Difficult Terrain, but you take [[/damage 1d10 Force]] damage if you end your turn inside a creature or an object.

Shadow Resilience. You have Resistance to all damage except Force and Radiant damage.
Umbral Form.name: Umbral Form

Symbiotic Agenda.name: Symbiotic Agenda
Symbiotic Agenda.condition: immediately after rolling a 1 on a D20 Test

Symbiotic Being.name: Symbiotic Being
Symbiotic Being.description:
A second being resides within your body, offering knowledge and assistance while furthering its own agenda. You gain the following features.

Entwined Existence. The symbiote can’t be targeted. If you die, so does your symbiote. If you are returned to life, your symbiote also revives.

Second Mind. You gain proficiency in one of the following skills: Arcana, Deception, History, Intimidation, Insight, Investigation, Nature, Religion, Perception, or Persuasion. You also know one additional language of your choice, chosen from the language tables in the Player’s Handbook.

Sustained Symbiosis. When you fail a saving throw, you can take a Reaction and expend one of your Hit Dice. Roll the die and add the number rolled to the saving throw, potentially turning the failure into a success.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Symbiotic Agenda. Immediately after you make a D20 Test and roll a 1 on the d20, your symbiote attempts to assert control. Make a Charisma saving throw (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you have the Charmed condition for [[/r 1d12#hours]] hours. While Charmed, you must try to follow the symbiote’s commands and further its goals, as determined by the DM. Whenever you take damage, you can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

At the DM’s discretion, you might make this saving throw whenever you act contrary to the symbiote’s agenda.

Symbiotic Agenda (Charmed).name: Symbiotic Agenda (Charmed)
Symbiotic Agenda (Charmed).description: You have the Charmed condition for 1d12 hours. While Charmed, you must try to follow the symbiote’s commands and further its goals, as determined by the DM. Whenever you take damage, you can repeat this save, ending the effect on a success.

Cursed (Path to the Grave).name: 被詛咒（歸墓之途）
Cursed (Path to the Grave).description:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwCSPathtotheCb inline]

攻擊檢定的劣勢不會自動套用。

End Curse Early.condition: 你或一個你的盟友命中被詛咒的目標時
End Curse Early.name: 提前終止詛咒

Path to the Grave.description:
作為一個附贈動作，你展示你的聖徽並消耗一次引導神力的使用次數，直到你的下一回合開始前，詛咒一個30呎範圍內你所能看見的生物。 在被詛咒期間，該生物的攻擊檢定與豁免檢定皆具有劣勢。

當你或一個你所能看見的盟友以攻擊檢定命中被詛咒的目標時，你可以提前終止詛咒（無需動作）並使本次攻擊額外造成等同於你牧師等級的額外[[/damage]]{暗蝕或光耀傷害}（由你選擇）。
Path to the Grave.name: 歸墓之途

Claim Departed Spirit.condition: when a visible target in range dies
Claim Departed Spirit.name: Claim Departed Spirit

Tokens of the Departed.name: Tokens of the Departed
Tokens of the Departed.description:
The spirits of the dead are drawn to you, and echoes of their past lives magically manifest as strange curios with resonant power.

You gain two @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwPRSoulTrinkg0]{soul trinkets}. A soul trinket is a Tiny object (the DM determines the trinket’s form or has you roll on the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.content.JournalEntry.phbCreatingAChar.JournalEntryPage.2nJWForKrOIXzELQ]{Trinkets} table in the Player’s Handbook to generate it). If you move more than 30 feet from a trinket, the trinket immediately teleports to you, appearing somewhere on your person.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwPRSoulTrinkg0 caption=false]

Gaining Additional Soul Trinkets. When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you dies, you can take a Reaction to gain another soul trinket, claiming a sliver of that creature’s departing spirit. The new trinket appears somewhere on your person.

You can have a maximum of two soul trinkets at a time. If you try to gain a soul trinket while at your maximum, one of your existing trinkets is immediately destroyed and replaced by the new trinket. The maximum number of soul trinkets you can have increases when you reach Rogue levels 13 (three trinkets) and 17 (four trinkets).

Whenever you finish a Long Rest with fewer than two soul trinkets, you gain soul trinkets until you have two.

Necrotic Husk.description:
Your connection to undeath saturates your body. You gain the following benefits.

Necrotic Resilience. You have Resistance to Necrotic damage. While using your Form of Dread, you have Immunity to Necrotic damage.

Unholy Resuscitation. If you drop to 0 Hit Points and don’t die outright, you can cause your body to erupt with deathly energy. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot Emanation originating from you makes a [[/save]]{Constitution saving throw} against your spell save DC, taking [[/damage]]{Necrotic damage} equal to 2d10 plus your Charisma modifier on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. Your Hit Points then change to twice your Warlock level, and you gain 1 Exhaustion level.

Once you use this benefit, you can’t use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.

Foundry Note

The benefits granted by Necrotic Resilience are integrated into the Form of Dread feature.

Unholy Resuscitation.condition: you drop to 0 HP and do not die outright
Unholy Resuscitation.name: Unholy Resuscitation

Necrotic Husk.name: Necrotic Husk

save.condition: a creature starts its turn within a 10' Emanation

Gaunt.description: Gaunt. The companion’s Speed increases to 45 feet, and it gains a Climb Speed equal to its Speed. It can climb difficult surfaces, including along ceilings, without needing to make an ability check. In addition, whenever a creature of your choice starts its turn within a 10-foot Emanation originating from your companion, the creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have the Frightened condition until the start of its next turn.

Frightened.name: Frightened
Frightened.description: The creature has the &Reference[frightened apply=false] condition until the start of its next turn.

Gaunt.name: Gaunt

Poisoned Roots.condition: within 10 miles of where you have Long Rested
Poisoned Roots.name: Poisoned Roots

Domain Traveler.condition: when you enter the Mists intent on reaching a domain
Domain Traveler.name: Domain Traveler

Mist Walk.name: Mist Walk
Mist Walk.condition: take damage or fail a saving throw to avoid or end the Grappled or Restrained condition

Mist Walker.name: Mist Walker
Mist Walker.description:
You know how to slip through the Mists’ grasp, but this freedom comes at a price: If you remain in one area for too long, the Mists find you and drain your life force. You gain the following features.

Domain Traveler. When you enter the Mists intent on reaching a specific domain, you are treated as if you possess a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwTheLandoftheM.JournalEntryPage.MistTalismansC63]{Mist talisman} keyed to that domain. To use this feature, you must know the name of the domain you have chosen as your destination, but you don’t need to have previously visited that land. This trait doesn’t allow you to bypass domain borders closed by a Darklord’s will.

Mist Walk. When you take damage or fail a saving throw to avoid or end the Grappled or Restrained condition, you can take a Reaction and teleport up to 15 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Poisoned Roots. When you finish a Long Rest, the world around you in a 10-mile radius becomes a siphon that leeches away at your vitality. Whenever you finish a Short Rest in that area, make a Constitution saving throw (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you get no benefits from finishing that rest.

Channeler.name: Channeler
Channeler.description:
You learn how to contact spirits beyond the grave, letting their power and knowledge flow through you. You gain the following benefits.

Guiding Whispers. You know the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplGuidance00]{Guidance} cantrip. It has a range of 60 feet when you cast it.

Spiritual Focus. You employ tools that aid you in channeling spirits. You gain a Gaming Set (@UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbgstPlayingcar]{playing cards}). You have proficiency with this Gaming Set, and you can use it or one of the following items as a Spellcasting Focus for your Bard spells: Arcane Focus (@UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbafcCrystal000]{crystal} or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbafcOrb0000000]{orb}), @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbagCandle00000]{Candle}, or @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.equipment.Item.phbagInkPen00000]{Ink Pen}.

Phantom.description:
Embrace Death and Wield Ghostly Power

Some Rogues traverse the veil between life and death, shepherding opponents to the grave and slipping through the world as undetectable as a spirit. In these pursuits, a Rogue might discover a mystical connection to death itself. Such an individual becomes immersed in negative energy, infusing their strikes with deathly energy and stealing knowledge from souls who have passed on. Thieves’ guilds value Rogues of this persuasion as highly effective information gatherers and spies.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.PhantomRogueBU0w]{Subclass Details}
Phantom.name: Phantom

Feat.title: Feat

Haunted One.name: Haunted One
Haunted One.description:
Ability Scores: Constitution, Wisdom, Charisma

Feat: @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwSurvivor2zrM3]{Survivor} or a @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice.

Skill Proficiencies: Arcana and Survival

Tool Proficiency: Choose one kind of Gaming Set

Equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Gaming Set (same as above), Crowbar, Holy Water (1 flask), Mirror, Oil (2 flasks), Signal Whistle, Tinderbox, Traveler’s Clothes, 5 Torches, Waterskin, and 14 GP; or (B) 50 GP

You are haunted by the events of your past. Whether it was a lone terrible incident or an accumulation of painful moments, you bear the unshakable weight of what happened to you. You’ve tried to bury it and run away from it, to no avail; it can’t be slain with a sword or banished via magic. Nevertheless, you persist.

College of Spirits.name: College of Spirits
College of Spirits.description:
Call Forth Spirits from Beyond the Grave

Bards of the College of Spirits conjure legendary spirits to change the world. But such entities are capricious, and what a Bard summons isn’t always entirely under their control.

@UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwSubclasses000.JournalEntryPage.CollegeofSpirits]{Subclass Details}

Second Skin.description:
There is another side of you that most people never see: a beast, a terrifying avenger, or a walking nightmare. You gain the following features.

Alternate Form. You always have the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplAlterSelf0]{Alter Self} spell prepared. You can cast it without a spell slot or spell components, and you must finish a Long Rest before you can cast it in this way again. You can also cast it using spell slots you have of the appropriate level. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this feat).

When you cast Alter Self without a spell slot using this feature, it doesn’t require Concentration.

Involuntary Change. Certain circumstances can involuntarily trigger your transformation. When you select this feat, roll on the Change Catalyst table to determine what triggers your change.

After you experience the catalyst, at the start of your next turn, make a [[/save]]{Charisma saving throw} (DC 13 plus your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, you immediately use Alternate Form to cast [[/item activity=ZrjtFFSbJZTj8Cfm]]{Alter Self} without a spell slot. If you’ve already expended the use of that feature, you instead have the &Reference[Stunned apply=false] condition until the start of your next turn.

@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.tables.RollTable.rhwChangeCatalys rollable resultLabel="Catalyst" classes="caption-top"]{Change Catalyst}

Involuntary Change.condition: at the start of your turn following experiencing the catalyst
Involuntary Change.name: Involuntary Change

Second Skin.name: Second Skin

Involuntary Change (Stunned).name: Involuntary Change (Stunned)
Involuntary Change (Stunned).description: If you’ve already expended the use of that feature, you instead have the &Reference[Stunned apply=false] condition until the start of your next turn.

Mist Wanderer.description:
Ability Scores: Dexterity, Constitution, Wisdom

Feat: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice (see “Feats” later in this chapter; @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwMistWalkerlge]{Mist Walker} is recommended)

Skill Proficiencies: Survival and Stealth

Tool Proficiency: Choose one kind of Artisan’s Tools

Equipment: Choose A or B: (A) Artisan’s Tools (same as above), Lamp, Oil (5 flasks), Pouch, Rope, Tinderbox, Traveler’s Clothes, and 30 GP; or (B) 50 GP

You once knew your home. But one day, the Mists rose and drew you into a Domain of Dread. Ever since, you have wandered between domains, braving the mercurial Mists as you search for a way home. Your time in these cursed realms has changed you, yet you find solace in communities of fellow wanderers throughout the Mists.

Choose Languages.title: Choose Languages
Choose Languages.hint: Your character knows at least three languages: Common plus two languages you roll or choose from the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.content.JournalEntry.phbCreatingAChar.JournalEntryPage.t1WP9VEevo4TOTXw#choose-languages]{Standard Languages} table.

Feat.title: Feat
Feat.hint: A @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.book.JournalEntry.rhwFeats00000000.JournalEntryPage.yEsfCxTXqE0ZGQ3B]{Dark Gift feat} of your choice (see “Feats” later in this chapter; @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwMistWalkerlge]{Mist Walker} is recommended)

Mist Wanderer.name: Mist Wanderer

Hungering Might.name: Hungering Might
Hungering Might.description:
You gain a bonus to Constitution saving throws equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of +1).

In addition, once per turn when you hit a creature with an attack roll while you are transformed using Wrath of the Wild, you regain a number of Hit Points equal to [[/heal 1d10+@abilities.wis.mod]]{1d10 plus your Wisdom modifier,} provided you are Bloodied when you hit.

Foundry Note

The once per turn healing activity is found on @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwHWWrathofth3C]{Wrath of the Wild} as a level-gated activity, Hungering Might.

Persistent Wrath.condition: reduced to 0 HP but not killed outright while transformed
Persistent Wrath.name: Persistent Wrath

Ominous Strikes.description: Ominous Strikes. When you hit a creature that has the &Reference[Frightened apply=false] condition with an attack roll, that attack deals extra damage equal to your Wisdom modifier.
Ominous Strikes.name: Ominous Strikes

Ancient Might.name: Ancient Might
Ancient Might.description:
You become wholly suffused with the wild’s ancient and terrible power, granting you the following benefits.

Ominous Strikes. When you hit a creature that has the &Reference[Frightened apply=false] condition with an attack roll, that attack deals extra damage equal to your Wisdom modifier.

Persistent Wrath. If you’re reduced to 0 Hit Points but not killed outright while transformed using @UUID[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwHWWrathofth3C]{Wrath of the Wild,} you can surge with wild power. Your Hit Points instead change to a number equal to twice your Ranger level. Once you use this feature, you can’t do so again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also expend a level 4+ spell slot (no action required) to restore your use of this feature.

Timeless. You have Immunity to the &Reference[Exhaustion apply=false] condition.

Foundry Note

The Ominous Strikes active effect should be toggled as needed depending on the attack target.

Beasts of Ill Omen.name: Beasts of Ill Omen
Beasts of Ill Omen.description:
You can call forth a howling creature of shadow to hound your foes. You can spend 3 Sorcery Points to cast @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSummonBeas]{Summon Beast} as a Bonus Action without expending a spell slot, without preparing the spell, and without Material components. The summoned creature appears as a beast made of shadow, and enemies within 5 feet of the summoned creature have Disadvantage on saving throws against spells you cast.

Whenever you cast the spell, you can modify it so that it doesn’t require Concentration. If you do so, the spell’s duration becomes 1 minute for that casting, and the spell ends early if you cast the spell again.

Drain.condition: damaging a target that is not a Construct or Undead
Drain.name: Drain

Strengthen.name: Strengthen
Strengthen.condition: damaging a target that is not a Construct or Undead

Vampiric Bite.name: Vampiric Bite
Vampiric Bite.description:
When you use your &Reference[Unarmed Strike] and deal damage, you can choose to bite with your fangs. You deal Piercing damage equal to [[/damage]]{1d4 plus your Constitution modifier} instead of the normal damage of an Unarmed Strike.

In addition, when you deal this damage to a creature that isn’t a Construct or an Undead, you can empower yourself in one of the following ways:

  • Drain. You regain Hit Points equal to the Piercing damage dealt.

  • Strengthen. You gain a bonus to the next ability check or attack roll you make within the next minute; the bonus is equal to the Piercing damage dealt.

You can empower yourself with this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

Foundry Note

The Active Effect provided by Strengthen includes the character's Constitution modifier, but not the damage roll nor modifications to total damage dealt due to resistance/vulnerability.

Strengthened Attack or Check.name: Strengthened Attack or Check
Strengthened Attack or Check.description: • You gain a bonus to the next ability check or attack roll you make within the next minute; the bonus is equal to the Piercing damage dealt.

Priest Spirit.name: Priest Spirit
Priest Spirit.description: The target [[/heal]]{regains Hit Points} equal to two rolls of your Bardic Inspiration die, and one of the following conditions of your choice ends on the target: &Reference[Blinded apply=false], &Reference[Charmed apply=false], &Reference[Deafened apply=false], &Reference[Paralyzed apply=false], &Reference[Poisoned apply=false], or &Reference[Stunned apply=false].

Attack and Save Disadvantage.name: Attack and Save Disadvantage
Attack and Save Disadvantage.description:
@Embed[Compendium.dnd-ravenloft-horrors-within.options.Item.rhwHowlA3f2I58L3 inline]

Disadvantage on attack rolls is not automated.

Howl.description:
As a Bonus Action, you let out an unearthly howl. Each creature of your choice within 15 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and Proficiency Bonus) or have Disadvantage on attack rolls and saving throws until the start of your next turn.

You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.
Howl.name: Howl

Empowered Channeling.description:
Your ability to channel spirits improves. You gain the following benefits.

Power from Beyond. Once per turn, when you cast a Bard spell with a spell slot that deals damage or restores Hit Points, roll 1d6. You gain a bonus equal to the number rolled to one of the spell’s damage rolls or to the total Hit Points the spell restores.

Spiritual Manifestation. You always have the @UUID[Compendium.dnd-players-handbook.spells.Item.phbsplSpiritGuar]{Spirit Guardians} spell prepared. You can cast the spell once without a spell slot, and you regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a Long Rest.

Whenever you cast the spell, you can modify it so the spirits also guard against worldly threats. When you cast the spell in this way, you and allies within the spell’s Emanation have Half Cover. Once you modify the spell in this way, you can’t do so again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
Empowered Channeling.name: Empowered Channeling

Spider Climb Improvement.description:
You have a Climb Speed equal to your Speed. When you reach character level 3, you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and along ceilings while leaving your hands free.

Foundry Note

The Climb Speed granted by this feature is provided by the species item directly.
Spider Climb Improvement.name: Spider Climb Improvement
